evals.prompt: Build me a REST API in Go that stores users in PostgreSQL. I need endpoints to create a user, get a user by ID, and list all users. Use best practices for project structure.
evals.expected_output: A Go project with cmd/server/main.go, internal packages, sqlc for database access, Chi router, goose migrations, proper error handling, and graceful shutdown.

assertions.text: Project includes a sqlc.yaml configuration file
Code imports github.com/go-chi/chi for HTTP routing
Contains SQL migration files with CREATE TABLE statements
SQL query files use sqlc annotations (:one, :many, :exec)
main.go handles OS signals for graceful shutdown
Project has cmd/ and internal/ directory structure
Handler/service functions accept context.Context as first parameter
Uses fmt.Errorf with %w for error wrapping

evals.prompt: Write a CLI tool in Go that reads a CSV file and outputs a summary with row count, column names, and the top 5 most common values in a specified column.
evals.expected_output: A Go CLI with flag parsing, CSV reading, summary logic, error handling to stderr, and table-driven tests.

assertions.text: Uses flag package for CLI argument parsing

evals.prompt: I need a generic cache with TTL in Go. It should be safe for concurrent use, support Get/Set/Delete, and automatically evict expired entries.
evals.expected_output: A generic Cache[K, V] struct using type parameters, sync.RWMutex for concurrency, background cleanup goroutine with context cancellation, and comprehensive tests.

assertions.text: Cache type uses type parameters [K comparable, V any]
Uses sync.RWMutex or sync.Mutex for concurrent safety
Has background goroutine for expired entry eviction
Implements Get, Set, and Delete methods
